title: #devex: 2487654321
preview:
Camila Ruiz: Heads-up — the new Astro edge starter used in the eval harness is flaky. Repro below, seems tied to edge fetch + auth-retry behavior.

Camila Ruiz: Local repro: clone examples/astro-edge-starter, pnpm install, start in edge emulation, set REDWOOD_TEST_EXPIRE_MS=900, call /api/stream. Stream truncates mi...
text:
Camila Ruiz: Heads-up — the new Astro edge starter used in the eval harness is flaky. Repro below, seems tied to edge fetch + auth-retry behavior.

Camila Ruiz: Local repro: clone examples/astro-edge-starter, pnpm install, start in edge emulation, set REDWOOD_TEST_EXPIRE_MS=900, call /api/stream. Stream truncates mid-response and eval marks it as regression.

Noah Bennett: I can reproduce on macos runner. Log shows: "StreamError: response body closed after 401 mid-read". Happens only in edge emulation, not Node dev.

Priya Singh: likely cause: in edge isolates we can't reliably share global refresh locks. Our JS SDK uses a single-process queue which works in Node but not in multi-isolate edge envs. So a mid-stream 401 causes one isolate to refresh while another keeps using stale token.

Ethan Park: snippet from src/routes/api/stream.ts:```js
export async function GET() {
 const s = aw...

title: #devex: 2301234567
preview:
Tara Lin: quick UX note — the new mini-preview row in the playground is promising but opening the schema still pushes the run history. during quick debugging that shift is distracting. can we avoid reflow?

Owen Gupta: Hmm, I ran into the same. Proposal: keep a fixed-height row for each fn with a 1-line summary (nam...
text:
Tara Lin: quick UX note — the new mini-preview row in the playground is promising but opening the schema still pushes the run history. during quick debugging that shift is distracting. can we avoid reflow?

Owen Gupta: Hmm, I ran into the same. Proposal: keep a fixed-height row for each fn with a 1-line summary (name + type badges + small chevron). expand should either layer an overlay anchored to that row or expand inline without changing page height.

Marta Ruiz: Overlay would preserve scroll position but may block copy/paste. Inline-but-no-reflow could work if the expansion overlays only the schema area (absolute positioned) and doesn't affect the DOM flow. that requires z-index management.

Ben Carter: agree with Marta. Also: button copy is inconsistent across the app — we have "Run", "Invoke", "Start". i vote for "Run" everywhere. small copy PR will be quick.

Tara Lin: a11y: scr...

text:
Alice Nguyen: Quick sync — we need to align the new function-calling + structured-output shape across Python/TS SDKs before we cut the beta. Proposing a compatibility shim approach so existing users aren't forced to upgrade runtime immediately. Thoughts?

Ben Carter: +1 on shim. Customers (two enterprise pilots) asked for typed function schemas and consistent error surface. Priority: no breaking changes for beta.

Maria Lopez: Agree on non-breaking. Docs ask: can we land example migrations and a short TOC for 'What changed' early? Marketing needs a one-pager.

Dan O'Connor: Technical constraints: serving runtime will return `tool_call` objects for tool invocations and `structured_output` as JSON string for older clients. Shim would parse/translate those into new typed shapes. Main risk is performance on large KV cache hits.

Evan Li: For TS types I can add a union: `ToolCallV1 | ToolC...

title: #devex: 1820001111
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick thread onboarding + SDK parity issue. New engineer did `redwood login` (writes ~/.redwood/token) then ran node sample and hit 401. Node SDK only checked REDWOOD_API_KEY. We should standardize precedence and improve diagnostics.

Priya Nair: Seen this pattern a few times. The inconsistency between...
text:
Jordan Blake: Quick thread onboarding + SDK parity issue. New engineer did `redwood login` (writes ~/.redwood/token) then ran node sample and hit 401. Node SDK only checked REDWOOD_API_KEY. We should standardize precedence and improve diagnostics.

Priya Nair: Seen this pattern a few times. The inconsistency between SDKs is the root cause. We need: clear precedence, a diagnostic whoami, and a deterministic rotation flow.

Marcus Lee: My proposal (minimal, backward-compatible): precedence = 1) explicit client creds passed to constructor, 2) REDWOOD_API_KEY (env), 3) REDWOOD_SERVICE_TOKEN (env; service/project scope), 4) REDWOOD_TOKEN_FILE (env path or default ~/.redwood/token), 5) OS keychain/secret stores. Keep REDWOOD_ORG / REDWOOD_PROJECT only for routing.

Tasha Rivera: +1. Also add an opt-in client.metadata.auth_source string so samples and CI can assert what was used. Example: `c...

title: #devex: 1805123456
preview:
Sam Park: hey team — working on my first PR into main. can someone quick me on the expected flow, branch naming, and any style/commit rules? also what CI should I expect to see? :pray:

Kevin Li: welcome Sam! TL;DR: branch from main, keep PRs small, run local lint/tests, request 1 reviewer from your area. infra/runt...
text:
Sam Park: hey team — working on my first PR into main. can someone quick me on the expected flow, branch naming, and any style/commit rules? also what CI should I expect to see? :pray:

Kevin Li: welcome Sam! TL;DR: branch from main, keep PRs small, run local lint/tests, request 1 reviewer from your area. infra/runtime changes need extra reviewers. :wave:

Olivia Chen: quick checklist I send new folks: 1) branch name: feature/<JIRA>-short-desc or fix/<JIRA>-short-desc (e.g. feature/ABC-123-add-healthcheck). 2) commit message header: <scope>: <short summary> (50 chars max). 3) run pre-commit and ruff/black. 4) include a short PR description + test plan. 5) add labels (area/runtime/docs).

Olivia Chen: sample commit and branch:
```
git checkout -b feature/ABC-123-add-telemetry
git commit -m "telemetry: add request duration metric for inference path"
```

Ben Carter: CI you'll see: lint ...

title: #devex: 1790123456
preview:
Nina Gomez: Heads up — after updating to redwood-inference-js@2.2.0 our SPA bundles jumped ~30KB and a few edge routes started failing with "TypeError: client.retry is not a function" in prod only. Local dev (pnpm dev) is fine.

Ethan Park: that sounds like an ESM/CJS interop issue combined with tree-shaking. Did yo...
text:
Nina Gomez: Heads up — after updating to redwood-inference-js@2.2.0 our SPA bundles jumped ~30KB and a few edge routes started failing with "TypeError: client.retry is not a function" in prod only. Local dev (pnpm dev) is fine.

Ethan Park: that sounds like an ESM/CJS interop issue combined with tree-shaking. Did you change how you import the SDK?

Nina Gomez: we changed to `import * as RW from 'redwood-inference-js'` to avoid ts2322 warnings. Then we do `const client = new RW.default({ apiKey })`.

Theo Martins: `import * as pkg` + `.default` is brittle across bundlers — sometimes the default export is a wrapper. Also if retry is attached to the prototype in a separate file and that file was flagged as side-effect-only it can be removed by tree-shaking.

Nina Gomez: looking at node_modules/redwood-inference-js/dist, the retry logic lives in `dist/strategies/retry.cjs.js` and package....

text:

title: #devex: 1768249973
preview:
Ibrahim Khan: Kicking off a quick thread on migration plan. We’re about to merge the standardized retry/timeout/auth + streaming semantics. Question: do we gate new defaults behind a feature flag (env var / client option) or ship as minor version defaults w/ deprecation warnings?

Monica Patel: +1. We need a call to...
text:
Ibrahim Khan: Kicking off a quick thread on migration plan. We’re about to merge the standardized retry/timeout/auth + streaming semantics. Question: do we gate new defaults behind a feature flag (env var / client option) or ship as minor version defaults w/ deprecation warnings?

Monica Patel: +1. We need a call today because release train is getting tight. My instinct: avoid breaking changes, but also avoid “three different behaviors forever”.

Dana Lo: TS perspective: we can ship as minor if old behavior remains accessible + we don’t change signatures. But some changes *are* behavior-breaking (e.g., retrying fewer things, timeouts being enforced more aggressively).

Sabrina James: Python: same. Biggest risk is people relying on “infinite-ish” timeouts in streaming (aka hung connections that never error). The new timeouts will surface errors earlier. That’s good but could surprise.
...

preview:

Ibrahim Khan: Key question: where does it live?
1) Dedicated r...
text:
Ibrahim Khan: Kicking off a thread on the conformance test suite design. We need one set of fixtures + “golden” expectations so Python/TS/Go all prove the same behavior for auth precedence, retry/backoff, timeouts, streaming disconnects, error taxonomy.

Ibrahim Khan: Key question: where does it live?
1) Dedicated repo (e.g. redwood-sdk-conformance) consumed by each SDK
2) One “source of truth” directory in one SDK repo, copied/synced
3) Duplicated fixtures per repo (least ideal)

Sahana Iyer: +1 to dedicated repo. CI-wise it’s easier to version/pin (each SDK can pin to a tag). If fixtures live in python repo, TS/Go CI would need cross-repo fetch anyway.

Dana Lo: Dedicated repo sounds clean. My only worry is the harness code is language-specific. If the conformance repo includes runner code, it’ll either become a monorepo or we’ll have 3 harnesses.

Mateusz Zielinski: I’d keep the co...

title: #devex: 1767789012
preview:
Ibrahim Khan: Seeing a cluster of reports about streaming disconnects behind corp proxies (Zscaler/Bluecoat-ish). Anyone else seeing this? Need to decide standard SDK behavior for “server went away mid-stream” vs “proxy buffered then resets”.

Owen Phillips: yup, SUP-29505 is basically that. Customer on enterprise n...
text:
Ibrahim Khan: Seeing a cluster of reports about streaming disconnects behind corp proxies (Zscaler/Bluecoat-ish). Anyone else seeing this? Need to decide standard SDK behavior for “server went away mid-stream” vs “proxy buffered then resets”.

Owen Phillips: yup, SUP-29505 is basically that. Customer on enterprise network, stream works on home wifi, breaks in office. They get partial tokens then the iterator just ends (no error) in Python. They assumed completion was “done”. :facepalm:

Sabrina James: Python currently treats EOF on the http stream as StopIteration w/o raising unless the parser hits invalid JSON first. We need to distinguish “graceful done” vs “socket closed mid-event”.

Dana Lo: TS side similar-ish. fetch stream reader returns `{ done: true }` and we stop; we only throw if we can’t parse an event. If proxy terminates cleanly, we think it’s done.

Mateusz Zielinski: Go...

title: #devex: 1767685544
preview:
Ibrahim Khan: Kicking off a quick review of retryable status codes + network error classification across SDKs. We’ve got drift between Python (requests/httpx), TS (fetch), Go (net/http). Want to lock a shared table today so conformance tests can encode it.

Ibrahim Khan: Current proposal (non-streaming): retry on 40...
text:
Ibrahim Khan: Kicking off a quick review of retryable status codes + network error classification across SDKs. We’ve got drift between Python (requests/httpx), TS (fetch), Go (net/http). Want to lock a shared table today so conformance tests can encode it.

Ibrahim Khan: Current proposal (non-streaming): retry on 408, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504 + network/transport errors. Do NOT retry on 400/401/403/404. Open question: 409/423/425? and what we consider a “network error” in each lang.

Sabrina James: +1. In python we currently have a mix:
- requests: `requests.exceptions.Timeout` and `ConnectionError` (includes DNS, refused, reset)
- httpx: `httpx.TimeoutException` vs `httpx.RequestError` (super broad)
Right now we accidentally retry some httpx RequestError cases that are actually local config (bad proxy) and it’s noisy.

Dana Lo: In TS it’s messy because `fetch` throws a `TypeError` for ...

title: #devex: 1767579921
preview:
Ibrahim Khan: Need to settle SDK default timeouts this week so we can finish ADR + start implementing consistently. Question is overall timeout (single budget) vs connect/read (granular). Also: how do we message the change to existing customers who might be relying on “no timeout” behavior in some libs.

Monica Pate...
text:
Ibrahim Khan: Need to settle SDK default timeouts this week so we can finish ADR + start implementing consistently. Question is overall timeout (single budget) vs connect/read (granular). Also: how do we message the change to existing customers who might be relying on “no timeout” behavior in some libs.

Monica Patel: +1. I keep hearing “Python times out but TS never does” and the opposite for streaming. We need a single story.

Allison Grant: SRE preference: overall timeout is easiest to reason about + prevents hanging sockets forever. But we still need a way to avoid retry storms when customers set it super low.

Dana Lo: In TS/fetch world, we basically have AbortController + a single timer. There isn’t a clean connect vs read split unless we swap HTTP stack (not happening). So “overall” maps well to TS.

Sabrina James: Python’s tricky because httpx exposes `connect/read/write/pool`...

title: #devex: 1751239876
preview:
Maya Singh: Hey folks — landing my first tiny PR to bump a handful of deps in package-builder. quick questions: branch naming, commit format, and any repo-specific checks I should run?

Ethan Park: Welcome Maya! Branches: <type>/<linear>-short-desc or <type>/<ticket>-short. We use chore for deps. e.g. chore/4287-bum...
text:
Maya Singh: Hey folks — landing my first tiny PR to bump a handful of deps in package-builder. quick questions: branch naming, commit format, and any repo-specific checks I should run?

Ethan Park: Welcome Maya! Branches: <type>/<linear>-short-desc or <type>/<ticket>-short. We use chore for deps. e.g. chore/4287-bump-psutil.

Maya Singh: Good to know. Commit message style? Do we keep multiple commits or squash?

Helen (eng): We prefer a single clean commit for dependency bumps. Use the conventional style: chore(deps): bump psutil 5.8.0 -> 5.9.1. If there were notable behavior changes add a short body.

Maya Singh: Where's the PR template and label guidance?

Ethan Park: PR template: .github/pull_request_template.md. Add labels: area/build and label:needs-review. For dep bumps include a migration/compat note if needed.

ProtoBot: FYI: If you modify any proto files, update generated fil...

text:
Talia Benson: Hey all — pushing the launch brief into "ready to execute" today. Need final owner assignments + dates for the remaining launch tasks so I can lock the checklist + comms. Please reply in-thread with what you own + ETA.

Scope reminders for v1:
- Python + TS RAG+agent templates
- CLI: `init` + `doctor` (no in-place upgrade story for v1)
- observability pack dashboards + recommended alerts
- docs IA + walkthrough + troubleshooting
- support enablement + announcement

Owner buckets we need to nail:
1) Docs updates (IA/nav, walkthrough, troubleshooting)
2) Dashboard pack (publish + verify)
3) Template release (repo tags, changelog, version stamps)
4) Support enablement (runbook, FAQ, escalation route)
5) Announcement (internal + external)

Also: any "must fix" blockers discovered in internal beta that are still open, please flag.

title: #devex: 1740831155
preview:
Monica Patel: Pulling together a list of SDK gaps that are actively blocking the quickstart templates (Python + TS) from feeling “production-minded by default”. Dropping here so we can track what’s must-fix before launch vs nice-to-have. Please add anything I missed.

Monica Patel: **TS SDK**
1) Streaming error type...
text:
Monica Patel: Pulling together a list of SDK gaps that are actively blocking the quickstart templates (Python + TS) from feeling “production-minded by default”. Dropping here so we can track what’s must-fix before launch vs nice-to-have. Please add anything I missed.

Monica Patel: **TS SDK**
1) Streaming error types are too lossy. In templates we can’t reliably tell `timeout` vs `connection reset` vs `429` vs `5xx` during stream. Right now it’s basically `Error: fetch failed` and we lose status/code.
2) No clean “stream aborted by client” vs “server closed stream” distinction.
3) Reconnect strategy for streaming: we need a safe default pattern (e.g., retry on network disconnect before any tokens, but do not replay partial responses). SDK doesn’t expose hooks/metadata to do this sanely.
4) Lack of a stable request-id capture. We need Redwood request id (gateway header) to show up in l...

title: #devex: 1740817442
preview:
Monica Patel: Kicking off a thread to track security review open items for Quickstart Templates (RAG+agents). Want to close these before we call internal beta “ready”. Please drop issues + owner + ETA here.

Marcus Lin: Thx. From the security pass today, top open items:
1) Secrets in logs: a couple places where exce...
text:
Monica Patel: Kicking off a thread to track security review open items for Quickstart Templates (RAG+agents). Want to close these before we call internal beta “ready”. Please drop issues + owner + ETA here.

Marcus Lin: Thx. From the security pass today, top open items:
1) Secrets in logs: a couple places where exceptions may print request headers/resp bodies. Need explicit redaction + test.
2) Sample data: current docs/sample corpus includes “realistic” customer-like content. We need it clearly synthetic + no PII. Also verify license/attribution.
3) Dependency pinning: Python + TS templates still allow floating ranges in a few deps. Need lockfiles + CI to enforce.
4) Redaction processors: OTel collector config needs processors to drop/redact sensitive attrs (headers, prompt content, tool args) by default.

Hanae Suzuki: +1 on (1). In the Python template I saw `logger.exception("reque...

title: #devex: 1740708451
preview: Monica Patel: quick q for @Allison Grant / @Logan Wright — we’re wiring OTel into the quickstart templates (RAG + agents) and want to make sure we don’t shoot ourselves w/ cardinality. what attrs/dimensions are “allowed” for spans + metrics? we have some ideas (model, template_version, tool_name, vector_store) but w...
text:
Monica Patel: quick q for @Allison Grant / @Logan Wright — we’re wiring OTel into the quickstart templates (RAG + agents) and want to make sure we don’t shoot ourselves w/ cardinality. what attrs/dimensions are “allowed” for spans + metrics? we have some ideas (model, template_version, tool_name, vector_store) but worried about exploding tags.

Allison Grant: +1 raising early. we’ve been burned before. general rule: spans can tolerate more context, metrics cannot. also: never put user input (prompt, query, doc IDs) into attrs.

Logan Wright: yep. metrics: keep label set small + bounded. if you can’t enumerate possible values in advance, it’s probably not safe for metrics.

Monica Patel: makes sense. current draft dims for metrics:
- `service.name` (template app)
- `deployment.mode` (hosted/dedicated/private)
- `model` (string)
- `http.route`
- `tool.name`
- `retriever.backend` (faiss/...

title: #devex: 1738874409
preview: Monica Patel: dropping the SDK “production defaults” proposal here for review. TL;DR: we’ve got too many first-prod launch issues that are basically missing timeouts/retries + weird streaming disconnect behavior. Proposal is to ship sane presets across Python/TS/Go and update quickstarts/snippets to use them. Questi...
text:
Monica Patel: dropping the SDK “production defaults” proposal here for review. TL;DR: we’ve got too many first-prod launch issues that are basically missing timeouts/retries + weird streaming disconnect behavior. Proposal is to ship sane presets across Python/TS/Go and update quickstarts/snippets to use them. Question: do we make these actual *defaults* or do we keep current behavior and expose an opt-in preset? Biggest worry is breaking folks who rely on “infinite” requests or who are doing their own retries.

Monica Patel: draft defaults (if we went that route):
- non-streaming: connect timeout 5s, request timeout 60s, retries: 2 (only on 408/409/429/5xx), backoff w/ jitter, respect `Retry-After`
- streaming: connect timeout 5s, no overall request timeout (or very high), but heartbeat / read timeout 30s, and reconnect helper (no auto-retry by default)
- also: emit `User-Agent: redwo...

title: #devex: 1688765432
preview:
maya rao: Heads up — console sparklines are showing weird quota spikes for a subset of tenants after yesterday's telemetry pipeline tweak. Looks like header normalization mismatch between older SDKs and the new ingest path.
jonathan li: saw this in dashboards. Which header is the culprit? route attribution or a samp...
text:
maya rao: Heads up — console sparklines are showing weird quota spikes for a subset of tenants after yesterday's telemetry pipeline tweak. Looks like header normalization mismatch between older SDKs and the new ingest path.
jonathan li: saw this in dashboards. Which header is the culprit? route attribution or a sampling flag?
sumi patel: it's the route attribution header. Some clients send `X-Route-Meta`, others `x-route-meta` and the pipeline normalization created duplicate buckets, so aggregation counts blew up for small tenants. :confused:
priya natarajan: oh no. We rolled a Python SDK change last week that normalizes casing — did we forget to coordinate with the ingest mapping?
luke chen: server-side expects case-insensitive, but the aggregation job partitions on the raw header string before normalization. So duplicates persist in the short window.
maya rao: proposal: (1) quick co...
